title: Awaited<Promise<T>> — resolves to the wrapped type
description:
TypeScript's built-in `Awaited

` utility unwraps the promise to its resolved type; tsgo resolves it at compile time, so this case lands as plain `string` in our cache and reuses the atomic string emit. The test verifies the utility threads through correctly.
notes:
`Awaited

` is resolved at the type-checker layer to the resolved value type — `Awaited>` becomes plain `string`. The validator is identical to the atomic-string emit; a real Promise does NOT satisfy it.
